PrimaryDetection: Server version plus isolated source access in TRY/CATCH
DefaultBehavior: Enabled for requested lightweight modules
Fallback: Return accessible/own-session data where possible and continue other modules

PrimaryDetection: Target database accessibility plus isolated source access
DefaultBehavior: Database-by-database best effort
Fallback: Skip only affected database/source and return warnings

PrimaryDetection: Database Query Store state and catalog availability
DefaultBehavior: Only when explicitly requested
Fallback: No historical claim; use current DMVs/plan cache only where appropriate

PrimaryDetection: DMV availability plus analysis-class policy
DefaultBehavior: Explicit call with independent object/time/row budgets
Fallback: Use targeted current-request context where available

PrimaryDetection: Database accessibility, metadata visibility and analysis-class policy
DefaultBehavior: Targeted mode by default; broad mode explicit
Fallback: Return technical identifiers and visible metadata only

PrimaryDetection: DMF availability plus analysis-class policy
DefaultBehavior: Explicit only; LIMITED scan mode default
Fallback: Skip physical details and keep catalog results

PrimaryDetection: Existing session/target plus runtime/catalog access
DefaultBehavior: Optional read-only source
Fallback: Use live DMVs/Query Store; unavailable history is not synthesized

PrimaryDetection: SQL Agent feature and msdb object visibility
DefaultBehavior: Optional infrastructure analysis
Fallback: Return server feature/status without inaccessible details

PrimaryDetection: Feature metadata, role and optional distributor visibility
DefaultBehavior: Optional; deep topology details explicitly gated
Fallback: Return available topology/status subsets

PrimaryDetection: Product major version 17+ and current database compatibility level 170+
DefaultBehavior: Used only for regex:/regexi: patterns
Fallback: Caller can use exact lists or like: patterns

PrimaryDetection: Trusted configuration plus validated dynamic identifiers and minimal probe
DefaultBehavior: Disabled until separately designed/configured
Fallback: Core analysis continues without external context

PrimaryDetection: External operating/security decision
DefaultBehavior: Not implemented by framework
Fallback: Operating organization may implement and re-sign after module changes

PrimaryDetection: msdb, database metadata and optional HADR/page visibility
DefaultBehavior: Read-only metadata; page details opt-in
Fallback: Return accessible evidence and state explicitly that absence is not proof

PrimaryDetection: Database-file metadata plus sys.dm_os_volume_stats
DefaultBehavior: Requested databases only
Fallback: Keep file and volume capacity separate; do not synthesize time-to-full

PrimaryDetection: Counter source plus supported cntr_type contract
DefaultBehavior: Snapshot by default; rate sample explicit
Fallback: Return unsupported types as RAW_UNINTERPRETED

PrimaryDetection: Existing system_health event-file target plus optional one-shot diagnostics
DefaultBehavior: Read-only event file; XML and sp_server_diagnostics opt-in
Fallback: Continue with the independently available source

PrimaryDetection: Product version, compatibility level, Query Store and tuning catalogs
DefaultBehavior: Opt-in; SQL 2022 views dynamically isolated
Fallback: Return configuration and explicit source availability without defect claim

PrimaryDetection: Server DMVs plus optional page metadata
DefaultBehavior: Interval sample and page details explicit
Fallback: Return cumulative or delta semantics and reset flag

PrimaryDetection: Memory DMVs and optional buffer descriptor scan
DefaultBehavior: Snapshot by default; buffer distribution opt-in
Fallback: Return lightweight memory evidence without automatic max-memory recommendation

PrimaryDetection: msdb backup and restore history
DefaultBehavior: Bounded visible-history window
Fallback: State pruned-history limitation and require test restore for proof

PrimaryDetection: Bounded statistics candidates, histogram DMF and incremental partition properties
DefaultBehavior: Explicit CATALOG_DEEP opt-in with a hard per-database candidate cap
Fallback: Return numeric evidence and limitations without plan-cause or out-of-range claims

PrimaryDetection: Compatibility level 130 plus child-module JSON contracts
DefaultBehavior: Core read-only modules enabled; expensive modules opt-in
Fallback: Return source module status and omit unavailable evidence

PrimaryDetection: Visible database catalogs plus server external-scripts configuration
DefaultBehavior: Lightweight aggregated catalog inventory; detected-only output optional
Fallback: Return per-database source status; zero visible rows never prove feature absence

PrimaryDetection: Visible XTP catalogs plus isolated database DMVs and resource-pool state
DefaultBehavior: Base sources on request; full hash-chain scan explicit and CATALOG_DEEP-gated
Fallback: Preserve accessible source results; no automatic DDL and no database attribution from the default pool

PrimaryDetection: Visible temporal catalogs plus approximate partition statistics
DefaultBehavior: Feature-gated per database; catalog mapping before isolated capacity and index sources
Fallback: Preserve accessible metadata; never infer row consistency, cleanup success or disconnected former mappings

PrimaryDetection: Visible Broker catalogs, approximate queue partitions and isolated activation DMVs
DefaultBehavior: Feature-gated per database; queue-dependent and independent runtime sources are isolated
Fallback: Preserve accessible metadata; never read queue payloads or infer a poison message from a disabled queue

PrimaryDetection: Visible Full-Text catalogs plus isolated population, batch, fragment, semantic, memory-pool and FDHost sources
DefaultBehavior: Feature-gated per database; server and database sources are isolated
Fallback: Preserve accessible metadata; never read indexed content, keywords, stopwords, crawl logs, paths or key values and never execute Full-Text DDL

PrimaryDetection: Visible Change Tracking and CDC catalogs plus isolated CDC DMV msdb and local Distributor sources
DefaultBehavior: CT and CDC are feature-gated per database; local replication details additionally require ENTERPRISE_TOPOLOGY_DEEP
Fallback: Require a consumer watermark for CT loss; preserve source gaps; never read change rows replication commands error texts credentials or agent commands

PrimaryDetection: TDE DMV, visible certificates, bounded backup metadata and aggregated Always Encrypted or Ledger catalogs
DefaultBehavior: Read-only and database-bounded; explicit backup-encryption expectation is opt-in
Fallback: Preserve source gaps; never read key paths signatures encrypted values media paths accounts or private keys

PrimaryDetection: Resumable-index catalog, current requests, ADR/PVS statistics and explicitly filtered Agent activity
DefaultBehavior: Read-only; Agent source is NOT_REQUESTED until a job filter is supplied
Fallback: Never read SQL text job steps commands messages identities clients or wait resources and never resume abort kill or clean up
